Title: Diurnal switches in diazotrophic lifestyle increase nitrogen contribution to cereals

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamine synthetase type I (GS), ATP-dependent ammonia-assimilating enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Klebsiella oxytoca / Escherichia coli / Salmonella typhimurium
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Klebsiella oxytoca / Escherichia coli / Salmonella enterica subsp. enterica serovar Typhimurium
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: K. oxytoca M5al; E. coli NCM3722; S. typhimurium SK3130 allele source
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Klebsiella oxytoca; Escherichia coli; maize; rice
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Klebsiella oxytoca; Escherichia coli; Zea mays; Oryza sativa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: K. oxytoca M5al/Ko424; E. coli NCM3722/Ec424; maize inbred line 93-63 (also screened B73, Fu8701); japonica rice
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native glnA promoter; engineered chromosomal mutant allele under native promoter
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ammonia assimilation / glutamine biosynthesis linked to biological nitrogen fixation control
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary ammonium assimilation branch interfacing with Ntr nitrogen regulation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream committed assimilation step and key control point connecting fixed ammonia to amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ATP-dependent condensation of ammonium with glutamate to form glutamine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Controls assimilation of fixed nitrogen and intracellular glutamine signal; reduced activity enables ammonia excretion rather than assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Fixed nitrogen delivered as ammonia/ammonium to cereals
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen nutrition supporting plant biomass increase and N accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizosphere/hydroponic medium and plant tissues including maize pheophytin nitrogen pool
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: glnA expression increased in Ec424 under ammonia-replete conditions via Ntr activation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anaerobic or microaerobic nitrogen-fixing conditions; temperature shift 23°C vs 30°C; ammonium supply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The GS P95L variant lowers intracellular glutamine, confers ammonium-tolerant nitrogen fixation, and at lower temperature uncouples nitrogen fixation from ammonia assimilation to promote ammonia excretion and plant nitrogen donation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A P95L substitution in GS encoded by glnA lowered intracellular glutamine while allowing growth under some ammonium-replete conditions. 2. The mutation caused constitutive/stronger activation of Ntr-regulated promoters and enabled ammonium-tolerant nitrogen fixation in engineered E. coli and K. oxytoca. 3. GS-P95L was thermosensitive, with lower activity at 23°C than 30°C, causing temperature-dependent ammonia excretion. 4. The K. oxytoca Ko424 strain improved nitrogen transfer to maize and rice under diurnal 30°C/23°C cycling, increasing biomass and plant N content.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth penalty under nitrogen-fixing conditions, stronger at lower temperature; severe nitrogen stress when constitutively excreting ammonia; reduced bacterial proliferation at constant 23°C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed chromosomal mutation; intracellular glutamine/glutamate quantification; promoter reporter assays; qRT-PCR; nitrogenase acetylene reduction assays; ammonia quantification; in vitro enzyme kinetics; 15N2 tracing by NMR and plant isotope labeling; plant inoculation phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering thermosensitive glnA alleles in associative diazotrophs offers a relatively simple strategy to create diurnal ammonia donation systems for cereals; can be tuned or stacked with colonization traits and genotype-matched host selection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The focal functional allele is glnA424 encoding GS-P95L. The applied effect in plants is mediated by engineered bacteria, not plant transgenesis. Deletion of glnE in Ko424 did not alter ammonia excretion, indicating phenotype is primarily due to low intrinsic GS-P95L activity/low glutamine rather than GlnE feedback.
